name: aegis-tools

metadata.name: aegis-tools
metadata.summary: Aegis is a set of battle-tested tools and tricks to help everyone make better software

documentation: A combination of tools and framework, Aegis has multiple different uses. You can import it and use the thoroughly made and tested functions. You can use it as a natural extension for the tornado web framework. And you can use it to quickly create a new web application with the structure already built-in, and follow along.

modules.name: aegis.build
documentation: It's really its own thing
Mainly a web interface to do a build. Command line backup/repair.
Python Imports

methods.name: aegis.build.Build.create
documentation: Create a new build

methods.name: aegis.build.Build.build_exec
documentation: Perform build and distribute to hosts. Do not use nice -n within build_exec, it adds to existing process. Set hydra niceness.

methods.name: aegis.build.Build._shell_exec
documentation: Shell execution with structured logging and database output handling.

methods.name: aegis.build.Build._done_exec
documentation: Do the final summary writes after the build step is done.

methods.name: aegis.build.Build._new_version
documentation: Version numbering and version files

methods.name: aegis.build.Build._incr_version
documentation: Increment minor (z) number up to 99 and the major numbers x and y to 10

methods.name: aegis.build.Build.commit_diff
documentation: Get the commits between two versions

methods.name: aegis.build.Build.start_deploy
documentation: Set previous version and send notifications

methods.name: aegis.build.Build.start_revert
documentation: Set to use previous version and send notifications

modules.name: aegis.hydra
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-
Hydra - The water monster of legend with multiple serpent heads on one body. This is the batch and worker system for downtime/background processing.
Main Thread is just a control. Hydra is the "batch" which checks what should run. HydraHeads are worker threads operating the hydra_queue.
Python Imports

methods.name: aegis.hydra.HydraThread.exception_alert
documentation: Alert and debug hooks likely need to be defined in applications

methods.name: aegis.hydra.HydraThread.signal_debug
documentation: Interrupt running process, and provide a stack trace for each thread. Trigger using kill -SIGUSR1 <pid>
Debug dump in response to kill -SIGUSR1

modules.name: aegis.stdlib
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
Python Imports

functions.name: aegis.stdlib.multi_shell
documentation: These processes should all be started and non-blocking. Then caller can use communicate(), wait(), call() as needed.

functions.name: aegis.stdlib.unique_list
documentation: From Maxime Biais's comment on http://www.peterbe.com/plog/uniqifiers-benchmark
Preserves order of the iterable in the return value

functions.name: aegis.stdlib.stable_intersection
documentation: From the accepted answer on http://stackoverflow.com/questions/642763/python-intersection-of-two-lists
Preserves order of the items in the second iterable

functions.name: aegis.stdlib.stable_difference
documentation: Modified from above - remove items in 'iter_remove' from items in 'iter_keep'

functions.name: aegis.stdlib.snake_to_camel
documentation: Tools for converting 'snake_case' to 'lowerCamelCase' 'UpperCamelCase' and 'Space Camel Case'. Adapted from: https://stackoverflow.com/a/19053800

functions.name: aegis.stdlib.json_snake_to_camel
documentation: This rewrites the key in-place. Make a copy.deepcopy() before calling this!

functions.name: aegis.stdlib.dt_to_ts
documentation:
calendar.timegm() doesn't maintain the microseconds awareness of datetime (!?!)
maintain the milliseconds as an integer to fit into 8 bytes: 1516687988867
could also for example be sent as decimal representation with microseconds: 1516687988.867397

functions.name: aegis.stdlib.is_robot
documentation: Test Robot with curl -X GET -kH 'User-Agent: Googlebot' https://<hostname>

functions.name: aegis.stdlib.rate_limit
documentation: Return True if should be rate-limited. Keep attribute on object passed in.

functions.name: aegis.stdlib.timer_start
documentation: Timer System for by-hand tracking _timings on any object

functions.name: aegis.stdlib.incr_version
documentation: Increment minor (z) number up to z_ct (use only 99 or 9) and the major numbers x and y to 10

classes.name: aegis.stdlib.EmailValidator

classes.name: aegis.stdlib.RobotValidator
documentation: ## Known Robots Handling, used along with user_agents module is_bot()

classes.name: aegis.stdlib.Memcache
documentation: Simple memory cache with expiry mechanism. Stores objects in process so no serialization required, but is not distributed over network.
The application must call purge_cache() as needed by the application to expire keys that haven't been accessed.

classes.name: aegis.stdlib.GeoLite
documentation: Support for MaxMind's free GeoLite2 database, sign up at https://www.maxmind.com/en/geolite2/signup
Add geoip2 to setup.py and pip requirements.txt, and set geolite_db_path to the filesystem location of the downloaded db in application config.py

methods.name: aegis.database.MysqlConnection.execute
documentation: Executes the given query, returning the lastrowid from the query.
rowcount is a more reasonable default return value than lastrowid,
but for historical compatibility execute() must return lastrowid.

methods.name: aegis.database.MysqlConnection.execute_lastrowid
documentation: Executes the given query, returning the lastrowid from the query.

methods.name: aegis.database.MysqlConnection.execute_rowcount
documentation: Executes the given query, returning the rowcount from the query.

methods.name: aegis.database.MysqlConnection.executemany
documentation:
Executes the given query against all the given param sequences.
We return the lastrowid from the query.

methods.name: aegis.database.MysqlConnection.executemany_lastrowid
documentation:
Executes the given query against all the given param sequences.
We return the lastrowid from the query.

methods.name: aegis.database.MysqlConnection.executemany_rowcount
documentation:
Executes the given query against all the given param sequences.
We return the rowcount from the query.
From torndb originally

methods.name: aegis.database.Row.kva_split
documentation: kva_split(), insert(), update() together are a mini-ORM in processing arbitrary column-value combinations on a row.
define table_name and data_columns to know which are allowed to be set along with user action
columns and where are simple dictionaries: {'full_name': "FULL NAME", 'email': 'email@example.com'}
A dict that allows for object-like property access syntax.

Aegis is your shield to protect you on the Brave New Web

functions.name: aegis.aegis_.create
documentation: ## Note to self: aegis create will work better if the core web is web.py so we don't clobber snowballin.py
Needs templates dir, etc
Prompt y/n to clobber with diff
Also need aegis install, to make system admin faster
Should all start with the /aegis stuff
Also the sql should all be there for hydra, reports, etc
Create a new spinoff of aegis

methods.name: aegis.webapp.AegisApplication.log_request
documentation:
From: <https://github.com/tornadoweb/tornado/blob/8afac1f805de738ccd0f58618b84b0a5f90dd346/tornado/web.py#L2114>
Writes a completed HTTP request to the logs. By default writes to the python root logger.  To change this behavior either:
subclass Application and override this method, or pass a function in the application settings dictionary as `log_function`.
## Overall Application Architecture
